2	import * as esbuild from 'esbuild'
3	import { BuildContext } from 'esbuild'
4	import { writeFileSync } from 'fs'
5	import glob from 'globby'
6	import path from 'path'
7	
8	import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
9	import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
10	import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
11	import { map } from '../blaze/map'
12	import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'
13	import { pipe } from '../blaze/pipe'
14	import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
15	import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
16	import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
17	import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
18	import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'
19	
20	export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
21	export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
22	  name?: string
23	  emitTypes?: boolean
24	  emitMetafile?: boolean
25	  outbase?: never // we don't support this
26	}
27	
28	const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
29	  platform: 'node',
30	  target: 'ES2022',
31	  logLevel: 'error',
32	  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
33	  metafile: true,
34	} as const
35	
36	/**
37	 * Apply defaults to the original build options
38	 * @param options the original build options
39	 */
40	const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
41	  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
42	  format: 'cjs',
43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
44	  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
45	  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
46	    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
47	  }),
48	  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
49	  ...options,
50	  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
51	  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
58	    onErrorPlugin,
59	  ],
60	  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
61	})
62	
63	/**
64	 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * We only want to trigger the glob search once we are ready, and that is when
82	 * the previous build has finished. We get the build options from the deferred.
83	 */
84	function computeOptions(options: () => BuildOptions) {
85	  return options()
86	}
87	
88	/**
89	 * Extensions are not automatically by esbuild set for `options.outfile`. We
90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */
92	function addExtensionFormat(options: BuildOptions) {
93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`
97	  }
98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`
104	 */
105	function addDefaultOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path
207	function getOutFile(options: BuildOptions) {
208	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)
210	    const filename = path.basename(options.outfile)
211	
212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []
232	}

Design a detailed implementation plan for adding a new Prisma generator package called `@prisma/generator-rest-api` that generates a typed REST API client from a Prisma schema. For each model it should generate TypeScript interfaces and CRUD methods (findMany, findUnique, create, update, delete) with proper types.

## Key Requirements
1. The generator must implement the standard Prisma `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator`
2. It should follow the existing monorepo package structure exactly
3. It should be registered in the generator registry
4. It should have proper tests

## Existing Architecture (from my exploration)

### Generator Interface (`packages/generator/src/generator.ts`)
```typescript
export type Generator = {
  readonly name: string
  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
  generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
}
```

### GeneratorOptions includes:
- `generator: GeneratorConfig` (has `output` with the target path)
- `dmmf: DMMF.Document` (the full schema data model)
- `datasources: DataSource[]`
- `datamodel: string` (raw schema text)

### DMMF structure (`packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`):
- `dmmf.datamodel.models: Model[]` — each has `name`, `fields: Field[]`
- `Field` has: `kind` ('scalar'|'object'|'enum'|'unsupported'), `name`, `type`, `isRequired`, `isList`, `isId`, `isUnique`, `hasDefaultValue`, `isReadOnly`, `isUpdatedAt`, relation fields
- `dmmf.datamodel.enums: DatamodelEnum[]` — each has `name`, `values: EnumValue[]`
- Scalar types: BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, Decimal, Float, Int, JSON, String

### Existing Generator Package Pattern (from `packages/client-generator-js/`):

**package.json** structure:
```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/client-generator-js",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": { ".": { "require": {...}, "import": {...} } },
  "dependencies": { "@prisma/dmmf": "workspace:*", "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*", ... },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build",
    "test": "vitest run --testTimeout 30000"
  }
}
```

**helpers/build.ts**: Uses `adapterConfig` from `helpers/compile/configs.ts` which creates CJS + ESM bundles from `src/index.ts`

**tsconfig.build.json**: `{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json", "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "dist", "rootDir": "src" }, "include": ["src"] }`

**tsconfig.json**: `{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.json", "include": ["./**/*"] }`

### Generator Implementation Pattern (from `packages/client-generator-js/src/generator.ts`):
```typescript
export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs  // = 'prisma-client-js'
  
  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    return {
      defaultOutput: './generated/rest-api-client',
      prettyName: 'Prisma REST API Client',
      version: clientVersion,
      requiresEngines: [],
    }
  }
  
  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
    // Generate files using dmmf
  }
}
```

### Generator Registry (`packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts`):
```typescript
export const defaultRegistry = new GeneratorRegistry()
defaultRegistry.add(new PrismaClientJsGenerator())
const tsGenerator = new PrismaClientTsGenerator()
defaultRegistry.add(tsGenerator)
defaultRegistry.addAliased('prisma-client', tsGenerator)
```

### BuiltInProvider (`packages/internals/src/built-in-provider.ts`):
```typescript
export const BuiltInProvider = {
  PrismaClientJs: 'prisma-client-js',
  PrismaClientTs: 'prisma-client',
} as const
```

### CI Testing (`test-template.yml`):
Generator tests run in the `client-packages` job alongside `client-generator-js`, `client-generator-ts`, `client-generator-registry`. Also mirrored in the `no-docker` job for Windows/macOS.

### `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`:
Maps package names to source paths for go-to-definition, e.g.:
```
"@prisma/client-generator-js": ["./packages/client-generator-js/src"],
```

## Design Decisions to Make

1. **Output format**: The generator should produce pure TypeScript files (interfaces + CRUD function stubs). The REST client should be self-contained — it provides a typed API client that users wire to their REST endpoints.

2. **Type mapping**: Map Prisma scalar types to TypeScript types:
   - String → string, Int/Float/Decimal → number, BigInt → bigint, Boolean → boolean, DateTime → Date, JSON → JsonValue, Bytes → Buffer

3. **CRUD methods per model**: For a `User` model, generate:
   - `findMany(params?: { where?, orderBy?, skip?, take? }): Promise<User[]>`
   - `findUnique(params: { where: UserWhereUniqueInput }): Promise<User | null>`
   - `create(params: { data: UserCreateInput }): Promise<User>`
   - `update(params: { where: UserWhereUniqueInput, data: UserUpdateInput }): Promise<User>`
   - `delete(params: { where: UserWhereUniqueInput }): Promise<User>`

4. **Input types**: Generate `UserCreateInput` (all fields except auto-generated ones like `@id` with `@default`, `@updatedAt`), `UserUpdateInput` (all create fields but optional), `UserWhereUniqueInput` (unique/id fields).

5. **Relations**: For relation fields (`kind: 'object'`), skip them in the REST client interfaces (REST APIs typically don't nest relations like GraphQL). Include the foreign key scalar fields.

6. **Enums**: Generate TypeScript string union types from Prisma enums.

7. **REST Client class**: Generate a main `RestApiClient` class that groups model methods, takes `baseUrl` in constructor, and uses `fetch` internally.

## Please Design

Create a detailed, step-by-step implementation plan covering:
1. All files to create and their contents (directory structure)
2. All existing files to modify  
3. The exact code generation logic (how DMMF models → TypeScript output)
4. Test strategy
5. Build/CI integration
6. Verification steps

input.content:
# Plan: Add `@prisma/generator-rest-api` Package

## Context

The goal is to add a new Prisma generator package that generates a typed REST API client from a Prisma schema. For each model, it produces TypeScript interfaces and CRUD methods (`findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete`) with proper types. The generator implements the standard Prisma `Generator` interface and integrates into the existing monorepo structure (build, registry, CI).

---

## New Package: `packages/generator-rest-api/`

### Directory Structure

```
packages/generator-rest-api/
  helpers/build.ts
  src/
    index.ts                          # Public exports
    generator.ts                      # Generator interface implementation
    generate.ts                       # Orchestrator: DMMF -> files on disk
    codegen/
      type-mapping.ts                 # Prisma scalar -> TS type map
      utils.ts                        # Shared helpers (field filtering, formatting)
      generate-enums.ts               # Enum -> union type codegen
      generate-model-interfaces.ts    # Model -> TS interface codegen
      generate-input-types.ts         # Create/Update/WhereUnique input codegen
      generate-client-class.ts        # RestApiClient class codegen
  tests/
    generator.test.ts                 # Integration tests (getGenerator + generate)
    codegen/
      generate-enums.test.ts
      generate-model-interfaces.test.ts
      generate-input-types.test.ts
      generate-client-class.test.ts
      type-mapping.test.ts
    fixtures/
      basic.prisma
      relations.prisma
      enums.prisma
      complex.prisma
  package.json
  tsconfig.build.json
  tsconfig.json
```

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1: Scaffold the package

Create `packages/generator-rest-api/` with config files:

- **`package.json`** — name `@prisma/generator-rest-api`, version `0.0.0`, standard exports (CJS/ESM/types), dependencies: `@prisma/dmmf`, `@prisma/generator`, `@prisma/internals` (all `workspace:*`), scripts: `dev`, `build`, `prepublishOnly`, `test` (vitest). Follow pattern from `packages/client-generator-js/package.json`.
- **`helpers/build.ts`** — `import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'; void build(adapterConfig)` (same as `packages/client-generator-js/helpers/build.ts`).
- **`tsconfig.build.json`** — extends `../../tsconfig.build.regular.json`, outDir `dist`, rootDir `src`, include `["src"]`.
- **`tsconfig.json`** — extends `../../tsconfig.json`, include `["./**/*"]`.

### Step 2: Implement codegen utilities

**`src/codegen/type-mapping.ts`**:
- Map Prisma scalars to TS types: `String->string`, `Int/Float/Decimal->number`, `BigInt->bigint`, `Boolean->boolean`, `DateTime->Date`, `Json->JsonValue`, `Bytes->Buffer`.
- Export `mapScalarType(prismaType: string): string` and a `JSON_VALUE_TYPE` string constant for the recursive `JsonValue` type definition.

**`src/codegen/utils.ts`**:
- `isScalarOrEnumField(field)` — returns true if `kind === 'scalar' || kind === 'enum'` (filters out relations).
- `isAutoGeneratedField(field)` — returns true if `isUpdatedAt`, `isGenerated`, `isReadOnly`, or `isId && hasDefaultValue` (excluded from CreateInput).
- `isUniqueIdentifierField(field)` — returns true if `isId || isUnique` (for WhereUniqueInput).
- `formatFieldType(tsType, field)` — wraps with `[]` if `isList`, appends `| null` if `!isRequired`.
- `lowerFirst(str)` — lowercase first character (for method naming).

### Step 3: Implement codegen modules

**`src/codegen/generate-enums.ts`** — `generateEnums(enums: readonly DMMF.DatamodelEnum[]): string | null`
- Returns `null` if no enums. For each enum, emit `export type Role = 'ADMIN' | 'USER'` (union types, not TS enums).

**`src/codegen/generate-model-interfaces.ts`** — `generateModelInterfaces(models, enums): string`
- For each model, emit `export interface User { ... }` with scalar/enum fields only (skip `kind: 'object'`).
- Import referenced enums from `./enums`. Include `JsonValue` type if any field uses `Json`.
- Use `mapScalarType` for scalars, enum name directly for enums. Apply `formatFieldType` for lists/nullability.

**`src/codegen/generate-input-types.ts`** — `generateInputTypes(models, enums): string`
- For each model, emit three interfaces:
  - **`UserCreateInput`**: scalar/enum fields excluding auto-generated ones. Fields with `hasDefaultValue` are optional. Required fields without defaults are required.
  - **`UserUpdateInput`**: same fields as CreateInput, all optional (partial update).
  - **`UserWhereUniqueInput`**: only `@id`/`@unique` fields (each optional since you provide one). Handle composite `@@id` via `model.primaryKey` and `@@unique` via `model.uniqueFields` as compound objects.

**`src/codegen/generate-client-class.ts`** — `generateClientClass(models): string`
- Emit a `FindManyParams<T>` generic interface with `where?`, `orderBy?`, `skip?`, `take?`.
- Emit `RestApiClient` class with:
  - Constructor: `{ baseUrl: string; headers?: Record<string, string> }`
  - Private `request<T>(path, init?)` helper using `fetch`, checks `response.ok`.
  - Private `toQueryString(params?)` helper for GET requests.
  - Per model (e.g., `User`), five methods: `userFindMany`, `userFindUnique`, `userCreate`, `userUpdate`, `userDelete` — each properly typed with the model interface and input types.
  - REST conventions: GET for reads, POST for create, PATCH for update, DELETE for delete.

### Step 4: Implement orchestrator

**`src/generate.ts`** — `generate(options: { dmmf, outputDir }): Promise<void>`
- Extract `dmmf.datamodel.models` and `dmmf.datamodel.enums`.
- Create `outputDir` recursively.
- Call each codegen function and write files: `enums.ts` (if enums exist), `models.ts`, `inputs.ts`, `client.ts`, `index.ts` (barrel re-export).

### Step 5: Implement generator class

**`src/generator.ts`** — `PrismaRestApiGenerator implements Generator`
- `name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaRestApi` (will be `'prisma-rest-api'`).
- `getManifest()`: returns `{ defaultOutput: './generated/rest-api-client', prettyName: 'Prisma REST API Client', requiresEngines: [] }`.
- `generate(options)`: resolves output dir via `parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)`, delegates to `generate()`.

**`src/index.ts`** — exports `PrismaRestApiGenerator` and `generate`.

### Step 6: Write tests

**Unit tests** (`tests/codegen/*.test.ts`):
- Test each codegen function in isolation with hand-crafted DMMF fragments (cast as the readonly types).
- Verify output via inline snapshots.
- Cover edge cases: empty models, all-optional fields, composite keys, Json/Bytes, enum-only schemas.

**Integration test** (`tests/generator.test.ts`):
- Use `getGenerator()` from `@prisma/internals` with an in-process registry (same pattern as `packages/client-generator-js/tests/generator.test.ts`).
- Fixture `.prisma` files for basic, relations, enums, and complex schemas.
- Call `generator.generate()`, verify output files exist and content matches snapshots.
- Verify manifest properties.

### Step 7: Register the generator

**`packages/internals/src/built-in-provider.ts`** — add `PrismaRestApi: 'prisma-rest-api'`:
```typescript
export const BuiltInProvider = {
  PrismaClientJs: 'prisma-client-js',
  PrismaClientTs: 'prisma-client',
  PrismaRestApi: 'prisma-rest-api',
} as const
```

**`packages/client-generator-registry/package.json`** — add dependency:
```json
"@prisma/generator-rest-api": "workspace:*"
```

**`packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts`** — import and register:
```typescript
import { PrismaRestApiGenerator } from '@prisma/generator-rest-api'
// ... existing code ...
defaultRegistry.add(new PrismaRestApiGenerator())
```

### Step 8: Update monorepo config

**`tsconfig.build.bundle.json`** — add path mapping for editor go-to-definition:
```json
"@prisma/generator-rest-api": ["./packages/generator-rest-api/src"]
```

**`.github/workflows/test-template.yml`** — add test step in `client-packages` job (after `client-generator-registry`) and in the `no-docker` job mirror:
```yaml
- run: pnpm run test
  name: 'generator-rest-api'
  working-directory: packages/generator-rest-api
```

---

## Files Modified (summary)

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `packages/internals/src/built-in-provider.ts` | Add `PrismaRestApi` entry |
| `packages/client-generator-registry/package.json` | Add `@prisma/generator-rest-api` dependency |
| `packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts` | Import and register `PrismaRestApiGenerator` |
| `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` | Add `@prisma/generator-rest-api` path mapping |
| `.github/workflows/test-template.yml` | Add test step in `client-packages` and `no-docker` jobs |

---

## Key Design Decisions

1. **Union types over TS enums** — `type Role = 'ADMIN' | 'USER'` is more idiomatic, has zero runtime cost, and works better with type narrowing.
2. **Relations excluded** — REST APIs don't nest relations like GraphQL. Only scalar/enum fields (including FK scalars like `authorId`) appear in interfaces.
3. **Method naming** — `{lowerModel}{Operation}` (e.g., `userFindMany`) avoids name collisions and keeps a flat client API.
4. **Minimal dependencies** — only `@prisma/dmmf`, `@prisma/generator`, `@prisma/internals`. No need for `ts-builders`, `engines-version`, etc.
5. **Plain string concatenation for codegen** — the output is simple enough that `ts-builders` would add unnecessary complexity. String arrays with `.join('\n')` are clear and testable.

---

## Verification

1. **Install**: `pnpm install` from root (links the new workspace package).
2. **Build**: `pnpm build` from root (builds all packages in dependency order via Turborepo).
3. **Unit tests**: `pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-api test` (runs Vitest).
4. **Integration test**: Same command — the integration tests use `getGenerator` with fixture `.prisma` files.
5. **Manual E2E**: Create a test schema with `generator rest { provider = "prisma-rest-api"; output = "./generated" }`, run `pnpm prisma generate`, verify the `generated/` directory contains `index.ts`, `models.ts`, `inputs.ts`, `client.ts` with correct types.
6. **Registry check**: Verify `prisma generate` recognizes `prisma-rest-api` as a built-in provider (no "unknown generator" error).

- Extract `dmmf.datamodel.models` and `dmmf.datamodel.enums`.
- Create `outputDir` recursively.
- Call each codegen function and write files: `enums.ts` (if enums exist), `models.ts`, `inputs.ts`, `client.ts`, `index.ts` (barrel re-export).

### Step 5: Implement generator class

**`src/generator.ts`** — `PrismaRestApiGenerator implements Generator`
- `name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaRestApi` (will be `'prisma-rest-api'`).
- `getManifest()`: returns `{ defaultOutput: './generated/rest-api-client', prettyName: 'Prisma REST API Client', requiresEngines: [] }`.
- `generate(options)`: resolves output dir via `parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)`, delegates to `generate()`.

**`src/index.ts`** — exports `PrismaRestApiGenerator` and `generate`.

### Step 6: Write tests

**Unit tests** (`tests/codegen/*.test.ts`):
- Test each codegen function in isolation with hand-crafted DMMF fragments (cast as the readonly types).
- Verify output via inline snapshots.
- Cover edge cases: empty models, all-optional fields, composite keys, Json/Bytes, enum-only schemas.

**Integration test** (`tests/generator.test.ts`):
- Use `getGenerator()` from `@prisma/internals` with an in-process registry (same pattern as `packages/client-generator-js/tests/generator.test.ts`).
- Fixture `.prisma` files for basic, relations, enums, and complex schemas.
- Call `generator.generate()`, verify output files exist and content matches snapshots.
- Verify manifest properties.

### Step 7: Register the generator

**`packages/internals/src/built-in-provider.ts`** — add `PrismaRestApi: 'prisma-rest-api'`:
```typescript
export const BuiltInProvider = {
  PrismaClientJs: 'prisma-client-js',
  PrismaClientTs: 'prisma-client',
  PrismaRestApi: 'prisma-rest-api',
} as const
```

**`packages/client-generator-registry/package.json`** — add dependency:
```json
"@prisma/generator-rest-api": "workspace:*"
```

**`packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts`** — import and register:
```typescript
import { PrismaRestApiGenerator } from '@prisma/generator-rest-api'
// ... existing code ...
defaultRegistry.add(new PrismaRestApiGenerator())
```

### Step 8: Update monorepo config

**`tsconfig.build.bundle.json`** — add path mapping for editor go-to-definition:
```json
"@prisma/generator-rest-api": ["./packages/generator-rest-api/src"]
```

**`.github/workflows/test-template.yml`** — add test step in `client-packages` job (after `client-generator-registry`) and in the `no-docker` job mirror:
```yaml
- run: pnpm run test
  name: 'generator-rest-api'
  working-directory: packages/generator-rest-api
```

---

## Files Modified (summary)

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `packages/internals/src/built-in-provider.ts` | Add `PrismaRestApi` entry |
| `packages/client-generator-registry/package.json` | Add `@prisma/generator-rest-api` dependency |
| `packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts` | Import and register `PrismaRestApiGenerator` |
| `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` | Add `@prisma/generator-rest-api` path mapping |
| `.github/workflows/test-template.yml` | Add test step in `client-packages` and `no-docker` jobs |

---

## Key Design Decisions

1. **Union types over TS enums** — `type Role = 'ADMIN' | 'USER'` is more idiomatic, has zero runtime cost, and works better with type narrowing.
2. **Relations excluded** — REST APIs don't nest relations like GraphQL. Only scalar/enum fields (including FK scalars like `authorId`) appear in interfaces.
3. **Method naming** — `{lowerModel}{Operation}` (e.g., `userFindMany`) avoids name collisions and keeps a flat client API.
4. **Minimal dependencies** — only `@prisma/dmmf`, `@prisma/generator`, `@prisma/internals`. No need for `ts-builders`, `engines-version`, etc.
5. **Plain string concatenation for codegen** — the output is simple enough that `ts-builders` would add unnecessary complexity. String arrays with `.join('\n')` are clear and testable.

---

## Verification

1. **Install**: `pnpm install` from root (links the new workspace package).
2. **Build**: `pnpm build` from root (builds all packages in dependency order via Turborepo).
3. **Unit tests**: `pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-api test` (runs Vitest).
4. **Integration test**: Same command — the integration tests use `getGenerator` with fixture `.prisma` files.
5. **Manual E2E**: Create a test schema with `generator rest { provider = "prisma-rest-api"; output = "./generated" }`, run `pnpm prisma generate`, verify the `generated/` directory contains `index.ts`, `models.ts`, `inputs.ts`, `client.ts` with correct types.
6. **Registry check**: Verify `prisma generate` recognizes `prisma-rest-api` as a built-in provider (no "unknown generator" error).

# Plan: Add `@prisma/generator-rest-api` Package

## Context

The goal is to add a new Prisma generator package that generates a typed REST API client from a Prisma schema. For each model, it produces TypeScript interfaces and CRUD methods (`findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete`) with proper types. The generator implements the standard Prisma `Generator` interface and integrates into the existing monorepo structure (build, registry, CI).

---

## New Package: `packages/generator-rest-api/`

### Directory Structure

```
packages/generator-rest-api/
  helpers/build.ts
  src/
    index.ts                          # Public exports
    generator.ts                      # Generator interface implementation
    generate.ts                       # Orchestrator: DMMF -> files on disk
    codegen/
      type-mapping.ts                 # Prisma scalar -> TS type map
      utils.ts                        # Shared helpers (field filtering, formatting)
      generate-enums.ts               # Enum -> union type codegen
      generate-model-interfaces.ts    # Model -> TS interface codegen
      generate-input-types.ts         # Create/Update/WhereUnique input codegen
      generate-client-class.ts        # RestApiClient class codegen
  tests/
    generator.test.ts                 # Integration tests (getGenerator + generate)
    codegen/
      generate-enums.test.ts
      generate-model-interfaces.test.ts
      generate-input-types.test.ts
      generate-client-class.test.ts
      type-mapping.test.ts
    fixtures/
      basic.prisma
      relations.prisma
      enums.prisma
      complex.prisma
  package.json
  tsconfig.build.json
  tsconfig.json
```

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1: Scaffold the package

Create `packages/generator-rest-api/` with config files:

- **`package.json`** — name `@prisma/generator-rest-api`, version `0.0.0`, standard exports (CJS/ESM/types), dependencies: `@prisma/dmmf`, `@prisma/generator`, `@prisma/internals` (all `workspace:*`), scripts: `dev`, `build`, `prepublishOnly`, `test` (vitest). Follow pattern from `packages/client-generator-js/package.json`.
- **`helpers/build.ts`** — `import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'; void build(adapterConfig)` (same as `packages/client-generator-js/helpers/build.ts`).
- **`tsconfig.build.json`** — extends `../../tsconfig.build.regular.json`, outDir `dist`, rootDir `src`, include `["src"]`.
- **`tsconfig.json`** — extends `../../tsconfig.json`, include `["./**/*"]`.

### Step 2: Implement codegen utilities

**`src/codegen/type-mapping.ts`**:
- Map Prisma scalars to TS types: `String->string`, `Int/Float/Decimal->number`, `BigInt->bigint`, `Boolean->boolean`, `DateTime->Date`, `Json->JsonValue`, `Bytes->Buffer`.
- Export `mapScalarType(prismaType: string): string` and a `JSON_VALUE_TYPE` string constant for the recursive `JsonValue` type definition.

**`src/codegen/utils.ts`**:
- `isScalarOrEnumField(field)` — returns true if `kind === 'scalar' || kind === 'enum'` (filters out relations).
- `isAutoGeneratedField(field)` — returns true if `isUpdatedAt`, `isGenerated`, `isReadOnly`, or `isId && hasDefaultValue` (excluded from CreateInput).
- `isUniqueIdentifierField(field)` — returns true if `isId || isUnique` (for WhereUniqueInput).
- `formatFieldType(tsType, field)` — wraps with `[]` if `isList`, appends `| null` if `!isRequired`.
- `lowerFirst(str)` — lowercase first character (for method naming).

### Step 3: Implement codegen modules

**`src/codegen/generate-enums.ts`** — `generateEnums(enums: readonly DMMF.DatamodelEnum[]): string | null`
- Returns `null` if no enums. For each enum, emit `export type Role = 'ADMIN' | 'USER'` (union types, not TS enums).

**`src/codegen/generate-model-interfaces.ts`** — `generateModelInterfaces(models, enums): string`
- For each model, emit `export interface User { ... }` with scalar/enum fields only (skip `kind: 'object'`).
- Import referenced enums from `./enums`. Include `JsonValue` type if any field uses `Json`.
- Use `mapScalarType` for scalars, enum name directly for enums. Apply `formatFieldType` for lists/nullability.

**`src/codegen/generate-input-types.ts`** — `generateInputTypes(models, enums): string`
- For each model, emit three interfaces:
  - **`UserCreateInput`**: scalar/enum fields excluding auto-generated ones. Fields with `hasDefaultValue` are optional. Required fields without defaults are required.
  - **`UserUpdateInput`**: same fields as CreateInput, all optional (partial update).
  - **`UserWhereUniqueInput`**: only `@id`/`@unique` fields (each optional since you provide one). Handle composite `@@id` via `model.primaryKey` and `@@unique` via `model.uniqueFields` as compound objects.

**`src/codegen/generate-client-class.ts`** — `generateClientClass(models): string`
- Emit a `FindManyParams<T>` generic interface with `where?`, `orderBy?`, `skip?`, `take?`.
- Emit `RestApiClient` class with:
  - Constructor: `{ baseUrl: string; headers?: Record<string, string> }`
  - Private `request<T>(path, init?)` helper using `fetch`, checks `response.ok`.
  - Private `toQueryString(params?)` helper for GET requests.
  - Per model (e.g., `User`), five methods: `userFindMany`, `userFindUnique`, `userCreate`, `userUpdate`, `userDelete` — each properly typed with the model interface and input types.
  - REST conventions: GET for reads, POST for create, PATCH for update, DELETE for delete.

### Step 4: Implement orchestrator

**`src/generate.ts`** — `generate(options: { dmmf, outputDir }): Promise<void>`
- Extract `dmmf.datamodel.models` and `dmmf.datamodel.enums`.
- Create `outputDir` recursively.
- Call each codegen function and write files: `enums.ts` (if enums exist), `models.ts`, `inputs.ts`, `client.ts`, `index.ts` (barrel re-export).

### Step 5: Implement generator class

**`src/generator.ts`** — `PrismaRestApiGenerator implements Generator`
- `name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaRestApi` (will be `'prisma-rest-api'`).
- `getManifest()`: returns `{ defaultOutput: './generated/rest-api-client', prettyName: 'Prisma REST API Client', requiresEngines: [] }`.
- `generate(options)`: resolves output dir via `parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)`, delegates to `generate()`.

**`src/index.ts`** — exports `PrismaRestApiGenerator` and `generate`.

### Step 6: Write tests

**Unit tests** (`tests/codegen/*.test.ts`):
- Test each codegen function in isolation with hand-crafted DMMF fragments (cast as the readonly types).
- Verify output via inline snapshots.
- Cover edge cases: empty models, all-optional fields, composite keys, Json/Bytes, enum-only schemas.

**Integration test** (`tests/generator.test.ts`):
- Use `getGenerator()` from `@prisma/internals` with an in-process registry (same pattern as `packages/client-generator-js/tests/generator.test.ts`).
- Fixture `.prisma` files for basic, relations, enums, and complex schemas.
- Call `generator.generate()`, verify output files exist and content matches snapshots.
- Verify manifest properties.

### Step 7: Register the generator

**`packages/internals/src/built-in-provider.ts`** — add `PrismaRestApi: 'prisma-rest-api'`:
```typescript
export const BuiltInProvider = {
  PrismaClientJs: 'prisma-client-js',
  PrismaClientTs: 'prisma-client',
  PrismaRestApi: 'prisma-rest-api',
} as const
```

**`packages/client-generator-registry/package.json`** — add dependency:
```json
"@prisma/generator-rest-api": "workspace:*"
```

**`packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts`** — import and register:
```typescript
import { PrismaRestApiGenerator } from '@prisma/generator-rest-api'
// ... existing code ...
defaultRegistry.add(new PrismaRestApiGenerator())
```

### Step 8: Update monorepo config

**`tsconfig.build.bundle.json`** — add path mapping for editor go-to-definition:
```json
"@prisma/generator-rest-api": ["./packages/generator-rest-api/src"]
```

**`.github/workflows/test-template.yml`** — add test step in `client-packages` job (after `client-generator-registry`) and in the `no-docker` job mirror:
```yaml
- run: pnpm run test
  name: 'generator-rest-api'
  working-directory: packages/generator-rest-api
```

---

## Files Modified (summary)

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `packages/internals/src/built-in-provider.ts` | Add `PrismaRestApi` entry |
| `packages/client-generator-registry/package.json` | Add `@prisma/generator-rest-api` dependency |
| `packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts` | Import and register `PrismaRestApiGenerator` |
| `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` | Add `@prisma/generator-rest-api` path mapping |
| `.github/workflows/test-template.yml` | Add test step in `client-packages` and `no-docker` jobs |

---

## Key Design Decisions

1. **Union types over TS enums** — `type Role = 'ADMIN' | 'USER'` is more idiomatic, has zero runtime cost, and works better with type narrowing.
2. **Relations excluded** — REST APIs don't nest relations like GraphQL. Only scalar/enum fields (including FK scalars like `authorId`) appear in interfaces.
3. **Method naming** — `{lowerModel}{Operation}` (e.g., `userFindMany`) avoids name collisions and keeps a flat client API.
4. **Minimal dependencies** — only `@prisma/dmmf`, `@prisma/generator`, `@prisma/internals`. No need for `ts-builders`, `engines-version`, etc.
5. **Plain string concatenation for codegen** — the output is simple enough that `ts-builders` would add unnecessary complexity. String arrays with `.join('\n')` are clear and testable.

---

## Verification

1. **Install**: `pnpm install` from root (links the new workspace package).
2. **Build**: `pnpm build` from root (builds all packages in dependency order via Turborepo).
3. **Unit tests**: `pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-api test` (runs Vitest).
4. **Integration test**: Same command — the integration tests use `getGenerator` with fixture `.prisma` files.
5. **Manual E2E**: Create a test schema with `generator rest { provider = "prisma-rest-api"; output = "./generated" }`, run `pnpm prisma generate`, verify the `generated/` directory contains `index.ts`, `models.ts`, `inputs.ts`, `client.ts` with correct types.
6. **Registry check**: Verify `prisma generate` recognizes `prisma-rest-api` as a built-in provider (no "unknown generator" error).

import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'

import { lowerFirst } from './utils'

/** Generates a typed RestApiClient class with CRUD methods for each model. */
export function generateClientClass(models: readonly DMMF.Model[]): string {
  const lines: string[] = ['// Generated by @prisma/generator-rest-api — do not edit', '']

  const modelImports = models.map((m) => m.name).join(', ')
  lines.push(`import type { ${modelImports} } from './models'`)

  const inputImports = models
    .flatMap((m) => [`${m.name}CreateInput`, `${m.name}UpdateInput`, `${m.name}WhereUniqueInput`])
    .join(', ')
  lines.push(`import type { ${inputImports} } from './inputs'`)
  lines.push('')

  lines.push('export interface FindManyParams<T> {')
  lines.push('  where?: Partial<T>')
  lines.push("  orderBy?: { [K in keyof T]?: 'asc' | 'desc' }")
  lines.push('  skip?: number')
  lines.push('  take?: number')
  lines.push('}')
  lines.push('')

  lines.push('export class RestApiClient {')
  lines.push('  #baseUrl: string')
  lines.push('  #headers: Record<string, string>')
  lines.push('')
  lines.push('  constructor(options: { baseUrl: string; headers?: Record<string, string> }) {')
  lines.push("    this.#baseUrl = options.baseUrl.replace(/\\/$/, '')")
  lines.push('    this.#headers = {')
  lines.push("      'Content-Type': 'application/json',")
  lines.push('      ...options.headers,')
  lines.push('    }')
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  lines.push('  async #request<T>(path: string, init?: RequestInit): Promise<T> {')
  lines.push('    const response = await fetch(`${this.#baseUrl}${path}`, {')
  lines.push('      ...init,')
  lines.push('      headers: { ...this.#headers, ...init?.headers },')
  lines.push('    })')
  lines.push('    if (!response.ok) {')
  lines.push('      throw new Error(`REST API error: ${response.status} ${response.statusText}`)')
  lines.push('    }')
  lines.push('    return response.json() as Promise<T>')
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  lines.push('  #toQueryString(params?: Record<string, unknown>): string {')
  lines.push("    if (!params) return ''")
  lines.push('    const searchParams = new URLSearchParams()')
  lines.push('    for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(params)) {')
  lines.push('      if (value !== undefined) {')
  lines.push("        searchParams.set(key, typeof value === 'object' ? JSON.stringify(value) : String(value))")
  lines.push('      }')
  lines.push('    }')
  lines.push('    const qs = searchParams.toString()')
  lines.push("    return qs ? `?${qs}` : ''")
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('')

  for (const model of models) {
    const name = model.name
    const lower = lowerFirst(name)
    const endpoint = `/${lower}`

    // findMany
    lines.push(`  async ${lower}FindMany(params?: FindManyParams<${name}>): Promise<${name}[]> {`)
    lines.push(`    const qs = this.#toQueryString(params as Record<string, unknown>)`)
    lines.push(`    return this.#request<${name}[]>(\`${endpoint}\${qs}\`)`)
    lines.push('  }')
    lines.push('')

    // findUnique
    lines.push(
      `  async ${lower}FindUnique(params: { where: ${name}WhereUniqueInput }): Promise<${name} | null> {`,
    )
    lines.push(`    const qs = this.#toQueryString(params.where as Record<string, unknown>)`)
    lines.push(`    return this.#request<${name} | null>(\`${endpoint}/unique\${qs}\`)`)
    lines.push('  }')
    lines.push('')

    // create
    lines.push(`  async ${lower}Create(params: { data: ${name}CreateInput }): Promise<${name}> {`)
    lines.push(`    return this.#request<${name}>(\`${endpoint}\`, {`)
    lines.push("      method: 'POST',")
    lines.push('      body: JSON.stringify(params.data),')
    lines.push('    })')
    lines.push('  }')
    lines.push('')

    // update
    lines.push(
      `  async ${lower}Update(params: { where: ${name}WhereUniqueInput; data: ${name}UpdateInput }): Promise<${name}> {`,
    )
    lines.push(`    return this.#request<${name}>(\`${endpoint}\`, {`)
    lines.push("      method: 'PATCH',")
    lines.push('      body: JSON.stringify({ where: params.where, data: params.data }),')
    lines.push('    })')
    lines.push('  }')
    lines.push('')

    // delete
    lines.push(`  async ${lower}Delete(params: { where: ${name}WhereUniqueInput }): Promise<${name}> {`)
    lines.push(`    return this.#request<${name}>(\`${endpoint}\`, {`)
    lines.push("      method: 'DELETE',")
    lines.push('      body: JSON.stringify(params.where),')
    lines.push('    })')
    lines.push('  }')
    lines.push('')
  }

  lines.push('}')
  lines.push('')

  return lines.join('\n')
}
